Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canadian Multiculturalism Act (non-application in Quebec)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Canadian Multiculturalism Act (non-application in Quebec)

Provision.Text: This bill was introduced during the first session of the 43rd Parliament. Pursuant to the Standing Orders of the House of Commons, it is deemed to have been considered and approved at all stages completed at the time of prorogation of the first session. The number of the bill remains unchanged.
This enactment amends the Canadian Multiculturalism Act to provide that it does not apply in Quebec.

Whereas Quebecers form a nation and therefore possess all the tools needed to define their identity and protect their common values, including as regards the protection of the French language, the separation of state and religion, and gender equality;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The Canadian Multiculturalism Act is amended by adding the following after section 2:
This Act does not apply in Quebec.
